g.title: cluster_3
g.text: Caveat 2 — Composition-only citations are common practice

g.title: cluster_4
g.text: Caveat 3 — The framework rewards strict matching, not clinical reasoning

a.xlink:title: A substantial fraction of ESMO HR+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer decision nodes is not directly supported by any trial-DAG edge that simultaneously matches patient state, biomarker definition, and temporal precedence; the gap is largest at post-CDK4/6i biomarker-stratified positions.
a.text: supported by any trial-DAG edge that
precedence; the gap is largest at

a.xlink:title: The pre-registered EFDPR rejected the 0.25 null under strict and ESCAT-aligned concordance.
Post-CDK4/6i biomarker-stratified positions emerged only after first-line CDK4/6i was adopted as standard of care, so the corresponding trials are still maturing.
a.text: standard of care, so the corresponding

a.xlink:title: Composing evidence across non-overlapping trials is a legitimate guideline-development practice that should not be penalised.
Under liberal tolerance accepting subgroup readouts, the lower CI bound touches the 0.25 threshold, so the rejection is fragile.
a.text: subgroup readouts, the lower CI bound

a.xlink:title: With only 14 pivotal trials and 15 decision nodes, both the EFDPR point estimate and its confidence interval are sensitive to single-edge changes.
a.text: decision nodes, both the EFDPR point

a.xlink:title: The strict 95% CI spans 0.267 to 0.733, a 0.47 wide interval, reflecting the small denominator.
Because the analysis was pre-registered before any outcome touching, post-hoc widening of the corpus would itself be a deviation requiring disclosure.
a.text: widening of the corpus would itself be

a.xlink:title: Under liberal tolerance the lower CI bound is 0.20, just below the 0.25 threshold, so the corpus is sensitive to tolerance choice.
a.text: Under liberal tolerance the lower CI
threshold, so the corpus is sensitive

a.xlink:title: Guideline committees routinely compose recommendations across non-overlapping trials when no single trial covers the exact decision node, especially in rapidly evolving fields.
a.text: trials when no single trial covers the

a.xlink:title: ESMO cites SOLAR-1 as supporting post-CDK4/6i PIK3CAmut recommendations, despite SOLAR-1 enrolling a post-AI (not post-CDK4/6i) population.
ESMO cites CAPItello-291 as supporting the post-CDK4/6i AKT-pathway node, although CAPItello-291 enrolled a mixed population in which prior CDK4/6i was permitted but not required.
a.text: CDK4/6i was permitted but not required.

a.xlink:title: Composing across non-overlapping trials assumes treatment effect transportability across patient subgroups, an assumption rarely tested formally.
Resistance mechanisms after CDK4/6i progression are distinct from de novo resistance, suggesting treatment-effect transportability is not automatic.
a.text: progression are distinct from de novo

a.xlink:title: The strict concordance algorithm is a deliberately conservative model that may over-flag clinically reasonable extrapolations as evidence-free.
a.text: The strict concordance algorithm is a

a.xlink:title: In clinical practice, extending SOLAR-1's PIK3CAmut signal from post-AI to post-CDK4/6i populations is considered a reasonable extrapolation, not an evidence gap.
The framework's value is precisely in making such extrapolations explicit and counting them, not in declaring them illegitimate.
a.text: The framework's value is precisely in
and counting them, not in declaring

a.xlink:title: The framework's three-tolerance output (strict / ESCAT / liberal) explicitly quantifies the dependence on extrapolation assumptions.
Every analytic choice (state encoding, biomarker token set, concordance algorithm, tolerance grouping) is materialised as a JSON file or a Python function and released under MIT licence.
a.text: materialised as a JSON file or a
Python function and released under MIT

a.xlink:title: The fourteen-trial structured-eligibility set, the fifteen-node ESMO encoding, and the concordance algorithm are all in the repository at the tagged commit.
a.text: concordance algorithm are all in the

a.xlink:title: The v1.0 schema is frozen; future extensions live in v1.1 and do not retroactively modify v1.0 fields.
a.text: The v1.0 schema is frozen; future
extensions live in v1.1 and do not

a.xlink:title: The primary outcome and its rejection threshold were committed before any outcome-touching analysis.
a.text: The primary outcome and its rejection

a.xlink:title: The same pipeline applies, without architectural change, to other tumour types whose guidelines compose multi-line decision trees across non-overlapping trials.
The (prior-state, biomarker) node space is generic; tumour-specific biomarker tokens are additive.
The concordance algorithm is biomarker-agnostic.
The same framework is planned for application to EGFR-mutant NSCLC and metastatic CRC with the ctDNA decision axis.
a.text: The same framework is planned for
metastatic CRC with the ctDNA decision

a.xlink:title: A committee that adopts this framework as a regular guideline-update audit can locate the specific decision nodes that lack supporting evidence chains and prioritise the next pivotal-trial agenda toward them.
a.text: A committee that adopts this framework
can locate the specific decision nodes

a.xlink:title: Each guideline node is a labelled object the committee already produces; the EFDPR overlay adds a single column to that artefact.
a.text: Each guideline node is a labelled
the EFDPR overlay adds a single column

a.xlink:title: Sponsors can use the list of evidence-free post-CDK4/6i biomarker-stratified nodes to design enrichment trials whose target population matches the guideline-recommended decision point.
a.text: Sponsors can use the list of

a.xlink:title: Three limitations bound the present study.
The 14-trial corpus is the documented pilot, not a systematic-review-level census of every HR+/HER2- mBC pivotal trial.
a.text: census of every HR+/HER2- mBC pivotal

a.xlink:title: The primary analysis uses ESMO 2024 as the guideline source; ASCO and NCCN sensitivity analyses are planned for an expansion.
a.text: The primary analysis uses ESMO 2024 as
the guideline source; ASCO and NCCN

a.xlink:title: Eligibility was hand-curated from primary publications. The LLM-extraction pipeline that will replace this for production is a separate technical contribution and is reported elsewhere.
a.text: replace this for production is a

a.xlink:title: The framework is released so that guideline committees, trial sponsors, and meta-research groups can extend it.
a.text: The framework is released so that
and meta-research groups can extend it.

a.xlink:title: A community-maintained Treatment Sequencing Evidence Atlas, updated at every ESMO and ASCO guideline release, could provide a continuously-refreshed EFDPR overlay across solid tumours.
a.text: every ESMO and ASCO guideline release,

a.xlink:title: Immediate priorities are the NSCLC and mCRC applications, an LLM-extraction validation paper with inter-rater agreement, and an interactive web viewer.
a.text: Immediate priorities are the NSCLC and
